proof: function[T0](x0: Set[T0]) { exists(k0: Nat -> T0) { forall(x1: T0) { not x1 ∈ x0 or exists(k1: Nat) { k0(k1) = x1 } } } = is_countable[T0](x0) }[Nat](Set.universal_set[Nat])
not exists(k0: Nat -> Nat) { forall(x0: Nat) { not x0 ∈ Set.universal_set[Nat] or exists(k1: Nat) { k0(k1) = x0 } } }
function(x0: Nat -> Nat) { not forall(x1: Nat) { not x1 ∈ Set.universal_set[Nat] or exists(k0: Nat) { x0(k0) = x1 } } }(f)
function(x0: Nat -> Nat) { exists(k0: Nat) { k0 ∈ Set.universal_set[Nat] and forall(x1: Nat) { x0(x1) != k0 } } }(f)
let w0: Nat satisfy { w0 ∈ Set.universal_set[Nat] and forall(x1: Nat) { f(x1) != w0 } }
let w1(arg0: Nat -> Nat) -> w1_result: Nat satisfy { w1_result ∈ Set.universal_set[Nat] and forall(x2: Nat) { arg0(x2) != w1_result } }
function(x0: Nat -> Nat) { w1(x0) ∈ Set.universal_set[Nat] and forall(x1: Nat) { x0(x1) != w1(x0) } }(f)
function(x0: Nat -> Nat) { forall(x1: Nat) { x0(x1) != w1(x0) } = true }(f)
function(x0: Nat -> Nat, x1: Nat) { w1(x0) != x0(x1) }(f, w1(f))
